steps.title: Read the sentence in context
steps.content: The underlined sentence describes a specific event: as a child, Yung wrote about an imaginary trip to New York in 1845, and later actually saw it. The very next sentence draws the general lesson: "sometimes our imagination foreshadows what lies uppermost in our minds..."

steps.title: Match the function
steps.content:
The underlined sentence is the specific example from Yung's life that illustrates the broader phenomenon stated right after. That's (C): an event that exemplifies a phenomenon.

(A), (B), and (D) misread the function — there's no fantasy-confusion, no publishing, no general adventurousness theme.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
